title: SQLite snapshot management for pseudo cache with validation
purpose: Manages writing, validating, and loading SQLite database snapshots of pseudo cache data, ensuring data integrity and schema compatibility.
module_context: Exports functions for writing snapshots to disk, validating existing snapshots against current project state, and loading snapshots into a database. Defines constants for snapshot tables, file count tolerance, sample size, TTL, and snapshot paths.

steps.content: Determine the snapshot file path within the project directory.
Ensure the snapshot directory exists, creating it if necessary.
Remove any existing snapshot file at the determined path.
Perform one-time cleanup of legacy snapshot file if not already done.
Attach the snapshot database file to the provided database connection.
Begin a transaction to ensure atomicity of the snapshot operation.
Drop and recreate each snapshot table in the attached database from the main database.
Insert or update the schema version in the snapshot metadata.
Insert or update the generation timestamp in the snapshot metadata.
Commit the transaction to finalize the snapshot write.
IF the write fails, rollback the transaction and detach the database.
Always detach the database connection in the finally block.

methods.name: validateSnapshot
methods.normalized_params: (snapPath: string, gitFileCount: number, sampleFiles: Map<string, string>): Promise<SnapshotValidation>

steps.content: Check if the snapshot file exists at the specified path.
IF the file does not exist, return validation result indicating missing snapshot.
Open the snapshot database in read-only mode for validation.
Perform an integrity check on the snapshot database.
IF the integrity check fails, return validation result indicating integrity issue.
Verify that the schema version in the snapshot matches the expected version.
IF the schema version does not match, return validation result indicating schema mismatch.
Count the number of files in the snapshot and compare it to the expected file count within tolerance.
IF the file count is outside the tolerance range, return validation result indicating file count mismatch.
Check the generation timestamp in the snapshot metadata.
IF the timestamp is missing or the snapshot is older than the TTL, return validation result indicating snapshot is too old.
Select a random sample of files from the provided sample files.
For each file in the sample, verify that the hash matches the one stored in the snapshot.
IF any hash does not match, return validation result indicating sample hash mismatch.
IF all validations pass, return validation result indicating the snapshot is valid.
Attach the snapshot database file to the provided database connection.
Begin a transaction to ensure atomicity of the load operation.
For each snapshot table, insert all rows from the attached snapshot database into the main database.
Commit the transaction to finalize the snapshot load.
IF the load fails, rollback the transaction and detach the database.
Always detach the database connection in the finally block.
